pullRequest.title: Scribe 2480 support
pullRequest.body:
- fix #460 

The first beta of Kindle Scribe full 2480 support is here. All important features work, including sleep covers.

The problem: The Kindle Scribe has a height of 2480 pixels. But kindlegen's limit is 1920. So images are always a little blurry compared to native resolution.

So how do you overcome this limitation?

The key idea: Instead of using a standard fixed layout epub where 1 image is 1 page, instead implement the alternate fixed layout epub where you can have multiple images per page. As long as each individual image is <1920, kindlegen is no issue.

Implementation wise, this was difficult because all the code assumed 1 image per page. If you look at the recent git commits to master, you'll see many of my commits doing an enormous refactor to account for this change.

This took several days between experimentation and testing, this was by far the largest feature I've ever worked on for KCC.

If you feel generous, please consider donating on my Ko-fi! It's taken a lot of my time and energy to make KCC as good as it is today. I've posted the test builds there too, but donation is not required. https://ko-fi.com/Post/Kindle-Scribe-full-2480-support-N4N31G6TQU

(if you decide to share this, please share the kofi link and not the direct link).

(Also looking for a software job in USA, lmk if you have leads: https://www.linkedin.com/in/alexu2/)

quick tip: try disabling image darkening with gamma = 1.0 if you previously used auto, things are much sharper so darkening is less neccessary.

pullRequest.title: Remove the rainbow effect on kaleido 3 by precisely attenuating the frequencies responsible instead of blurring the image
pullRequest.body:
Highly effective removal of rainbow effect artifacts on Kaleido 3 displays by significantly attenuating the responsible frequencies in the spectral domain (>= 0.3 cycles/pixel, oriented around 135°).
This method avoids blurring the entire image by precisely targeting problematic frequencies (at the cost of a ~ 18% processing time increase vs the blurring method on processed images).

Fixes #771

Early‑return in `slugify()` when `options.format == 'CBZ'`, so KCC keeps the
original (Unicode) folder names instead of transliterating them.

```python
def slugify(value):
    if options.format == 'CBZ':
        return value
    # existing code…

Fixes to grayscale pages in forcecolor mode
pullRequest.body:
Fixes bit depth/quantization for grayscale pages with forcepng + forcecolor options.
Now color pages will be saved in 8bit truecolor (24bit per pixel) and grayscale pages in 8bit (2bit/4bit/8bit dynamically based on given quantization palette after https://github.com/ciromattia/kcc/pull/976 is merged)

- Closes https://github.com/ciromattia/kcc/issues/932

Slight optimization to JPEG grayscale pages with forcecolor option.
Consistently results in ~0.7% reduction in output file size.

pullRequest.title: Improved color detection
pullRequest.body:
Improves the color detection algorithm to better handle images that contain only small regions of color but have a low overall color-to-pixel ratio. Previously, such images were often misclassified as grayscale, even when they did contain color. With this change, these pages are now correctly identified as color, reducing false negatives in color detection.

Additionally, this PR makes the color check run only when it’s actually needed, instead of running it up front every time. This avoids doing extra work if the color check isn’t used, which reduces unnecessary computation.

- Closes https://github.com/ciromattia/kcc/issues/995

pullRequest.title: upgrade from p7zip to sevenzip on macOS/Linux
only slugify cbz subfolders if sort matters
pullRequest.body:
- fix #1006 

Only slugify if the sort order would change.

If it’s already 01 02 it won’t change it. Since that will sort properly anywhere 

It would only change if it was like 9 10 because some devices will sort 10 before 9. Then it would become 09 10

Thanks for your input.

pullRequest.title: Fix makeZIP function to allow for special characters in folder names
pullRequest.body:
Linux (and probably macOS) filesystems allow for folders with special characters in it.  This appears to cause issues when a folder that contains a question mark is passed to 7zip within the makeZIP function.  Since ? is considered a wildcard character, 7zip ends up adding additional folders to the zip file, which results in an epub with incorrect structure.

To solve this, we can pass `cwd=basedir` within subprocess_run so that 7zip is executed within basedir, and have 7zip archive everything within that folder.

This should 
- fix #1085

pullRequest.title: Added file fusion to C2E
Optimized Docker image
pullRequest.body:
Closes #1154 
### Refactor Docker build process for simplicity and optimization

This pull request overhauls the Docker build process to follow modern best practices, resulting in a simpler, faster, and more secure build that produces smaller final images.
Summary of Changes:

Unified Dockerfile:

- The separate Dockerfile-base has been eliminated and its logic merged into a single, multi-stage Dockerfile.
- This removes the need for a two-step build process and a separate docker-base-publish.yml GitHub workflow, which has been deleted. The entire image is now built atomically.

Optimized Multi-Stage Build:

- The new Dockerfile uses a proper multi-stage pattern with a builder stage and a final stage.
- The builder stage installs all build-time dependencies (build-essential, -dev packages, etc.).
- The final stage starts from a clean base image and only copies necessary runtime dependencies and application code from the builder, significantly reducing the final image size and attack surface.

Flexible Entrypoint Script:

- A new entrypoint.sh script has been introduced to act as the container's entrypoint.
- This allows for greater flexibility, enabling different modes of operation (running the c2e or c2p) controlled by the KCC_MODE environment variable.

Dependency Optimization:

- A new requirements-docker.txt has been created.
- PySide6 and raven have been removed from this list as they are GUI-specific dependencies and are not required for the headless CLI environment in the Docker container.
- The .dockerignore file has been updated to exclude unnecessary files, ensuring a clean and efficient build context.

Build Process Improvements:

- The build logic now leverages Docker's layer caching more effectively by copying requirements-docker.txt and installing dependencies before copying the application source code.
- Removed apt-get upgrade in favor of relying on the base image maintainers for security patches, ensuring build reproducibility.

Testing:

The new Docker image has been successfully built and tested (superficially, as there are no automated tests available) on the following architectures:

- linux/amd64: Works as expected.
- linux/arm64: Works as expected.

Testing for linux/arm/v7 was attempted, but the build process under QEMU emulation was prohibitively slow due to the heavy compilation required for some Python packages on this architecture.
